title: Iranian Supertanker HUGE Evades US Naval Blockade Carrying $220 Million in Crude Oil to Asia
summary: TankerTrackers.com reported that NITC supertanker HUGE (IMO 9357183) evaded the US Navy blockade and reached the Asia Pacific carrying over 1.9 million barrels of crude oil, currently traversing Indonesia's Lombok Strait.
contents:
## NITC Supertanker Breaks Through US Blockade

### Evasion Details
TankerTrackers.com identified a National Iranian Tanker Company (NITC) VLCC supertanker that successfully evaded the US naval blockade and reached the Far East. The vessel, named HUGE (IMO 9357183), carried over 1.9 million barrels of crude oil valued at nearly $220 million.

### Tracking Data
The vessel transmitted its last AIS signal on March 20, 2026, when it departed the Strait of Malacca bound for Iran. TankerTrackers last spotted HUGE off Sri Lanka over a week ago. The supertanker currently traverses Indonesia's Lombok Strait toward the Riau Archipelago — indicating a Chinese destination.

### Broader Evasion Pattern
Iranian state media claimed at least 52 Iranian vessels breached the US-imposed blockade within a 72-hour period, including 31 oil tankers and 21 cargo vessels. The US military maintains it intercepted or forced approximately 41 Iran-linked ships to turn back since the blockade began.

### Sanctions Enforcement Challenge
The successful evasion undermines Trump's claim that Washington has the Strait of Hormuz under "total control." The HUGE's 47-day dark voyage demonstrates continued Iranian capability to circumvent the naval blockade through AIS transponder manipulation and alternative routing through Indonesian waters.

**Sources:** TankerTrackers.com via X, Times of India, Fars News Agency
